title: Kaolin clay face mask
subtitle: A gentle dry-mix clay mask for all skin types with colloidal oat
excerpt: A dry clay mask blend made from kaolin clay and colloidal oatmeal with optional lavender essential oil. Mix a small amount with rose water at the point of use, apply for 10 minutes, and rinse. The dry mix keeps for 12 months; once water is added, use immediately. Suitable for all skin types including sensitive.
sourceNotes: Formulators Kitchen kaolin clay properties (formulatorskitchen.com, accessed May 2026): kaolin as the mildest cosmetic clay, suitable for all skin types. Colloidal oatmeal skin soothing properties: Kurtz and Wallo, Colloidal oatmeal in dermatology (Journal of Drugs in Dermatology, 2007).

content.text: A dry-mix clay mask has a practical advantage over pre-made wet masks: it keeps for months in a sealed jar and you mix only as much as you need per use. Kaolin is the mildest of the cosmetic clays, with a gentle
action that suits sensitive and dry skin as well as oily. Colloidal oatmeal adds a soothing, swelling-calming quality that offsets any tightening from the clay.
Mix about one tablespoon of the dry powder with enough rose water to reach a smooth

that spreads evenly across the face without dragging. Leave on for 10 minutes and rinse thoroughly with warm water before the clay fully dries; clay left to crack on the skin draws moisture from the surface. Lavender essential oil is optional; leave it out for an unscented version or for particularly sensitive skin.

items.name: Kaolin clay
items.prepNote: Cosmetic-grade white kaolin clay. The finest grade available gives the best slip.

items.name: Colloidal oatmeal
items.prepNote: Very finely milled oat powder. Not porridge oats, which are too coarse for a face mask.

items.name: Lavender essential oil
items.prepNote: Optional. Mix directly into the dry clay powder; the clay absorbs and disperses the EO through the batch. Lavandula angustifolia.

content.text: Weigh the kaolin clay and colloidal oatmeal into the small mixing bowl. If using lavender essential oil, add it drop by drop and stir until the clay absorbs it evenly with no visible oil pooling. Transfer to a sealed glass jar and label with the making date.

Measure approximately 1 tablespoon of dry mix into a small dish. Add rose water a teaspoon at a time, stirring between each addition, until the paste reaches a smooth spreading consistency. Apply to clean skin, avoiding the eye area. Leave for 10 minutes, then rinse thoroughly with warm water. Pat dry. Use a clean, dry spoon each time to scoop from the storage jar.

Store the dry mix in a sealed glass jar away from steam and moisture. Keep out of the bathroom if possible; humidity will cause the clay to clump and may introduce bacteria. The dry mix keeps for 12 months. Never store the hydrated paste. If the powder smells stale or the lavender scent has completely faded, make a fresh batch.
